    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 933
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíErdŇĎs asked whether the limsup of the ratio of the {2,3}-smooth part of n(n+1)
    23‚Üíto n¬∑log(n) is infinite.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/933](https://www.erdosproblems.com/933)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Er76d] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems in number theory and combinatorics_ (1976).
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen Real
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos933
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/--
    35‚ÜíThe $\{2,3\}$-smooth part of a natural number $n$, i.e., $2^{v_2(n)} \cdot 3^{v_3(n)}$,
    36‚Üíwhere $v_p$ denotes the $p$-adic valuation.
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def smoothPart23 (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    39‚Üí  2 ^ padicValNat 2 n * 3 ^ padicValNat 3 n
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 933 [Er76d]:
    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíIf $n(n+1) = 2^k \cdot 3^l \cdot m$ where $\gcd(m, 6) = 1$, is it true that
    45‚Üí$$\limsup_{n \to \infty} \frac{2^k \cdot 3^l}{n \log n} = \infty?$$
    46‚Üí
    47‚ÜíEquivalently: for every $C > 0$, there exist arbitrarily large $n$ such that
    48‚Üíthe $\{2,3\}$-smooth part of $n(n+1)$ exceeds $C \cdot n \cdot \log n$.
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíMahler proved that $2^k \cdot 3^l < n^{1+o(1)}$.
    51‚ÜíErdŇĎs wrote that 'it is easy to see' that for infinitely many $n$,
    52‚Üí$2^k \cdot 3^l > n \cdot \log n$.
    53‚Üí-/
    54‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    55‚Üítheorem erdos_933 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    56‚Üí    ‚ąÄ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚Üí
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ n : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č§ n ‚ąß
    58‚Üí      (smoothPart23 (n * (n + 1)) : ‚ĄĚ) > C * (n : ‚ĄĚ) * Real.log (n : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    59‚Üí  sorry
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üíend Erdos933

    33‚Üí/-- An integer $m$ is $x$-smooth if all its prime factors are at most $x$. -/

    37‚Üí/-- The set of $n$ such that $n+1, n+2, \ldots, n+k$ are all $x$-smooth. -/

    39‚Üí  {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, 1 ‚Č§ i ‚Üí i ‚Č§ k ‚Üí IsSmooth x (n + i)}
    40‚Üí

    45‚Üí    ((S ‚ą© {i | 1 ‚Č§ i ‚ąß i ‚Č§ N}).ncard : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• őī

    47‚Üí/--

    29‚Üíin the sense that the density of $n$ satisfying both conditions equals
    30‚Üí$\rho(1/\alpha) \cdot \rho(1/\beta)$, where $\rho$ is the Dickman function.

    33‚Üíis $\rho(1/\alpha)$.

    36‚Üí$\rho(1/\alpha) \rho(1/\beta)$.

    38‚ÜíElliott‚ÄďHalberstam conjecture for friable integers.

    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[Te18] Ter√§v√§inen, J., *On binary correlations of multiplicative functions*. Forum Math.

    24‚ÜíFor $0 \le k \le n$ write $\binom{n}{k} = uv$ where the only primes dividing $u$ are in $[2,k]$

    30‚ÜíA heuristic suggests $f(n) \sim 2 \log n$ for most $n$.

    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- $f(n)$ for ErdŇĎs Problem 684: the smallest $k$ such that the $k$-smooth part
    48‚Üíof $\binom{n}{k}$ exceeds $n^2$. Returns $0$ if no such $k$ exists. -/

    25‚Üíin any $A \subseteq \{1,\ldots,n\}$ of size $|A| = k$ there exist primes $p_1,\ldots,p_r$ such
    26‚Üíthat more than $r$ elements $a \in A$ are only divisible by primes from $\{p_1,\ldots,p_r\}$.

    35‚Üí/-- The prime counting function $\pi(n)$: the number of primes $\leq n$. -/

    37‚Üí  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter Nat.Prime).card

    40‚Üíprime factor of $a$ belongs to $P$. -/

    42‚Üí  ‚ąÄ p : ‚Ąē, Nat.Prime p ‚Üí p ‚ą£ a ‚Üí p ‚ąą P
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $f(k,n)$ is the smallest integer $r$ such that in any $A \subseteq \{1,\ldots,n\}$ of size
    45‚Üí$|A| = k$, there exist $r$ primes $p_1,\ldots,p_r$ such that more than $r$ elements of $A$ are
    46‚Üíonly divisible by primes from $\{p_1,\ldots,p_r\}$. -/

    48‚Üí  sInf {r : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ a ‚ąß a ‚Č§ n) ‚Üí A.card = k ‚Üí
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÉ P : Finset ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ p ‚ąą P, Nat.Prime p) ‚ąß P.card = r ‚ąß
    50‚Üí      ‚ąÉ B : Finset ‚Ąē, B ‚äÜ A ‚ąß B.card > r ‚ąß ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą B, IsSmoothWrt b P}
    51‚Üí

    55‚ÜíIs it true that $2\pi(\sqrt{n}) - f(\pi(n)+1, n) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$?

    58‚Üí$f(\pi(n)+1, n) + M \leq 2\pi(\lfloor\sqrt{n}\rfloor)$.

    62‚Üí    answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ

    64‚Üí      smoothCoveringNumber (primeCounting n + 1) n + M ‚Č§ 2 * primeCounting (Nat.sqrt n) := by

    67‚Üíend Erdos983

    21‚Üí/-!
    22‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 33
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/33](https://www.erdosproblems.com/33)
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen Classical

    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/-- Let `A ‚äÜ ‚Ąē` be a set such that every integer can be written as `n^2 + a` for some `a` in `A`
    33‚Üíand `n ‚Č• 0`. -/
    34‚Üí-- Formalisation note: Changed 'every large integer' to 'every integer' as for the statement these
    35‚Üí-- conditions are equivalent. Also, this was the formulation in the original paper `by Erdos.
    36‚Üídef AdditiveBasisCondition (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=

    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- Let `A ‚äÜ ‚Ąē` be a set such that every integer can be written as `n^2 + a`

    45‚Üí    (A.1.interIcc 1 N).ncard / (‚ąöN : EReal)) = answer(sorry) := by

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 122
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/122](https://www.erdosproblems.com/122)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíFor which arithmetic functions $f$ is it true that the count of $n$ with $n + f(n)$ in a
    25‚Üíshort interval $(x, x + F(x))$, divided by $F(x)$, is unbounded? ErdŇĎs‚ÄďPomerance‚ÄďS√°rk√∂zy
    26‚Üíproved this for $f = \tau$ (divisor count) and $f = \omega$ (distinct prime factor count).
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[Er97] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some of my new and almost new problems and results in combinatorial
    29‚Üínumber theory* (1997).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Er97e] ErdŇĎs, P., *Some problems and results on combinatorial number theory* (1997).
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[EPS97] ErdŇĎs, P., Pomerance, C., and S√°rk√∂zy, A., *On locally repeated values of certain
    34‚Üíarithmetic functions, III* (1997).
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen Filter
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Erdos122
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- The count of natural numbers $n$ such that $n + f(n)$ falls in the open real
    44‚Üíinterval $(x, x + F(x))$. Since $n \le n + f(n) < x + F(x)$ requires $n < x + F(x)$,
    45‚Üíit suffices to search $n$ in the range $[0, x + \lceil F(x) \rceil]$. -/
    46‚Üínoncomputable def shiftCount (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (F : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) (x : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    47‚Üí  ((Finset.range (x + ‚ĆąF x‚ĆČ‚āä + 1)).filter
    48‚Üí    (fun (n : ‚Ąē) => (x : ‚ĄĚ) < (n : ‚ĄĚ) + (f n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß
    49‚Üí              (n : ‚ĄĚ) + (f n : ‚ĄĚ) < (x : ‚ĄĚ) + F x)).card
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- The natural density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is zero: the proportion of elements
    52‚Üíin $\{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ belonging to $A$ tends to $0$ as $N \to \infty$. -/
    53‚Üídef HasNaturalDensityZero (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    54‚Üí  Tendsto
    55‚Üí    (fun N : ‚Ąē => ((Finset.range N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card / (N : ‚ĄĚ))
    56‚Üí    atTop (nhds 0)
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- $f(n)/F(n) \to 0$ for almost all $n$ in the natural-density sense:
    59‚Üífor every $\varepsilon > 0$, the set $\{n : f(n)/F(n) \ge \varepsilon\}$ has natural
    60‚Üídensity zero. -/
    61‚Üídef AlmostAllRatioVanishes (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (F : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ) : Prop :=
    62‚Üí  ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí HasNaturalDensityZero {n : ‚Ąē | őĶ ‚Č§ (f n : ‚ĄĚ) / F n}
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/-- The ErdŇĎs-122 property of $f$: for any positive $F$ with $f(n)/F(n) \to 0$
    65‚Üíin natural density, the ratio $\#\{n \in \mathbb{N} : n+f(n) \in (x, x+F(x))\} / F(x)$
    66‚Üíis unbounded as $x \to \infty$ (equivalently, its limsup equals $+\infty$). -/
    67‚Üídef HasErdos122Property (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    68‚Üí  ‚ąÄ F : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚ĄĚ, (‚ąÄ n, 0 < F n) ‚Üí AlmostAllRatioVanishes f F ‚Üí
    69‚Üí    ‚ąÄ C : ‚ĄĚ, ‚ąÉŠ∂† x : ‚Ąē in atTop, C < (shiftCount f F x : ‚ĄĚ) / F x
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí/--
    72‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem #122 [Er97, Er97e, EPS97]
    73‚Üí
    74‚ÜíFor which number-theoretic functions $f : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{N}$ is it true that for any
    75‚Üípositive function $F : \mathbb{N} \to \mathbb{R}$ satisfying $f(n)/F(n) \to 0$ for almost all $n$
    76‚Üí(in the natural density sense), there are infinitely many $x$ for which
    77‚Üí$$\#\{n \in \mathbb{N} : n + f(n) \in (x, x + F(x))\} / F(x) \to \infty?$$
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíErdŇĎs, Pomerance, and S√°rk√∂zy [EPS97] proved that this holds when:
    80‚Üí- $f(n) = \tau(n)$ = number of divisors of $n$, and
    81‚Üí- $f(n) = \omega(n)$ = number of distinct prime divisors of $n$.
    82‚Üí
    83‚ÜíErdŇĎs believed the property to be FALSE for:
    84‚Üí- $f(n) = \varphi(n)$ (Euler's totient function), and
    85‚Üí- $f(n) = \sigma(n)$ (sum of divisors).
    86‚Üí
    87‚ÜíThe full characterization of which $f$ satisfy `HasErdos122Property` remains open.
    88‚ÜíThe theorem below records the two proved instances.
    89‚Üí-/
    90‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    91‚Üítheorem erdos_122 :
    92‚Üí    HasErdos122Property (fun n => (Nat.divisors n).card) ‚ąß
    93‚Üí    HasErdos122Property (fun n => (Nat.primeFactorsList n).toFinset.card) := by
    94‚Üí  sorry
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üíend Erdos122

    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 609

    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/609](https://www.erdosproblems.com/609)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $f(n)$ be the minimal $m$ such that if the edges of $K_{2^n+1}$ are coloured
    25‚Üíwith $n$ colours then there must be a monochromatic odd cycle of length at most $m$.
    26‚ÜíEstimate $f(n)$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs and Graham [ErGr75]. The edges of $K_{2^n}$ can be
    29‚Üí$n$-coloured to avoid monochromatic odd cycles entirely (partition vertices by
    30‚Üíbinary coordinates). Day and Johnson [DaJo17] proved $f(n) \to \infty$ and
    31‚Üí$f(n) \geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log n}}$ for some $c > 0$. Janzer and Yip [JaYi25] proved
    32‚Üí$f(n) \leq O(n^{3/2} \cdot 2^{n/2})$.
    33‚Üí

    45‚Üíconsecutive edges $v_0 v_1, v_1 v_2, \ldots, v_{k-1} v_0$ have the same color. -/

    27‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., _Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    28‚Üítheory_. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).

    35‚Üí/-- The $t$-smooth component of $n$: the product of $p^{v_p(n)}$ over all
    36‚Üí    primes $p < t$ dividing $n$. -/

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1146
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíErdŇĎs asked whether the set of 3-smooth numbers {2^m ¬∑ 3^n : m, n ‚Č• 0} is an essential component
    23‚Üíwith respect to Schnirelmann density.
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1146](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1146)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Va99] Ruzsa, I. Z., *Sumsets and structure*, Combinatorial Number Theory and Additive
    28‚ÜíGroup Theory (1999).
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen Classical
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos1146
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--
    36‚ÜíThe Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$, defined as
    37‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{n \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}.$$
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    40‚Üí  ‚®Ö n : ‚Ąē+, (((Finset.Icc 1 (n : ‚Ąē)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ)
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/--
    43‚ÜíThe sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ for sets of natural numbers.
    44‚Üí-/
    45‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    46‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/--
    49‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an essential component if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    50‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üídef IsEssentialComponent (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (B : Set ‚Ąē), 0 < schnirelmannDensity B ‚Üí schnirelmannDensity B < 1 ‚Üí
    54‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) > schnirelmannDensity B
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/--
    57‚ÜíThe set of 3-smooth numbers: $\{2^m \cdot 3^n \mid m, n \geq 0\}$.
    58‚Üí-/
    59‚Üídef smoothNumbers23 : Set ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  {k | ‚ąÉ m n : ‚Ąē, k = 2 ^ m * 3 ^ n}
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/--
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 1146 [Va99, 1.19]:
    64‚ÜíIs the set $B = \{2^m \cdot 3^n : m, n \geq 0\}$ an essential component?
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an essential component if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    67‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    68‚Üí

    70‚Üíthe collection of numbers in the form $2^m \cdot 3^n$ and ErdŇĎs often asked whether
    71‚Üíit is an essential component or not."
    72‚Üí-/
    73‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    74‚Üítheorem erdos_1146 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ IsEssentialComponent smoothNumbers23 := by
    75‚Üí  sorry
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üíend Erdos1146

    36‚Üípower factors $p^a$ where $a \geq 2$. -/

    38‚Üí  (n.factorization.support.filter (fun p => 2 ‚Č§ n.factorization p)).prod

    53‚Üí      (powerfulPart (‚ąŹ i ‚ąą Finset.range (‚Ąď + 1), (n + i)) : ‚ĄĚ) <
    54‚Üí        (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ ((2 : ‚ĄĚ) + őĶ) := by
    55‚Üí  sorry

    60‚ÜíFor every $\ell \geq 2$, $\limsup_{n \to \infty} Q_2(n(n+1)\cdots(n+\ell))/n^2 = \infty$.
